text:
Your Amazon main image is your ad.
Not your infographic. Not your A+ content.
Your main image is the only thing a shopper sees before deciding to click or scroll.
We tested 3 variants for a supplement brand. The winner had zero text, white background, product front-facing.
CTR went from 4.2% to 7.8%. Same price. Same PPC spend.
Stop designing. Start testing.

I analyzed 200 Amazon listings that went from under $10k/month to over $100k/month.
One thing they all changed first: the main image.
Not the title. Not the bullets. Not the price.
The image.

An agency quoted my client $18,000 for a full Amazon listing redesign.
7 images, lifestyle shots, A+ content.
6 week timeline.
We did it with AI in 40 minutes.
The client couldn't tell which was which in a blind test.

Unpopular opinion: most Amazon sellers are optimizing the wrong thing.
You're split-testing bullet points while your main image has a 2% CTR.
Fix the image first. Always.

The brands winning on Amazon in 2025 have one thing in common.
They treat their listing like a landing page, not a product catalogue.
Every image has a job. Main image: stop the scroll. Image 2: explain the problem. Image 3: prove the solution. Image 4: handle objections. Image 5: social proof.
